dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6991 IH: Fair Trade Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Van Duyne introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To impose additional duties on goods imported into the United States.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Trade Act of 2026.

section.header: Imposition of additional duties on goods imported into United States

subsection.text: With respect to goods imported into the United States, for each calendar year beginning on or after the date of the enactment of this Act, there shall be imposed the following duties:

paragraph.text: 10 percent ad valorem of each good imported from a country with which the United States has a trade surplus.
15 percent ad valorem of each good imported from a country with which the United States has a trade deficit.

subsection.text: Each duty required under subsection (a) shall be in addition to any other duty imposed by law with respect to the good.

paragraph.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the President may reduce the percentage requirement in subsection (a) to an amount greater than zero percent with respect to any good if the President determines that such reduction is in the national interest or national security interest of the United States.

Prior to exercising authority under paragraph (1), the President shall consult with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate prior to determine whether such exercise of authority is appropriate.
